text:
Market today: Chips are getting hit hard - Samsung and SK Hynix both dumped over 9% overnight as a semiconductor rout spreads from Wall Street. That's the real driver today. Your sector (SMH, SOXL, chip names) is under direct pressure. Oil falling and Iran talk progress is macro noise for you; the chip headline is what actually matters.

Your holdings: SOXL (-14.52%) is your loudest alarm - it's a 3X leveraged ETF decaying against you, sitting only ~8% above its $200 stop. On margin, that gap shrinks fast. SPAL (-13.72%) is a brand-new, very volatile leveraged IPO with almost zero cushion above its $21.38 stop - margin amplifies this. SMH (-2.5%) is grinding lower with the sector; watch that $540 stop.

Watch: Whether SMH holds above $540 as the chip rout news digests. SOXL's $200 stop is the line in the sand.

Bottom line: Protect capital first - your two leveraged losers (SOXL, SPAL) need stop discipline today, not hope.
plays: SOXL: Stop at $200 is non-negotiable given the 3X leverage, -14.5% hole, and a live chip rout today. Consider trimming now if you're uncomfortable with that stop distance on margin.
SPAL: Stop $21.38 is basically right here. Either tighten it manually or be ready to exit fast - this leveraged IPO has no room and margin makes it worse.
No new buys today - chip sector under active pressure, scanner setups are long-biased into a headwind. Hold and watch.
